ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Belaa o bol o kɔlɔŋ bɛl Kooro Abemɛlɛk bonɔɔna aa kpɔ kpeɡri lɛ la waa baɡlo.
translated: It is white, big, and very long. King Abimelech's people saw it and were terrified.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Asɛ Abraham chɔɔ daa bɛl Beɛseeba bela, dɛɛ tomɛ Korowii waa lole saŋ bɛlbwa la bela.
translated: And Abraham dug a well in Beersheba, and he planted a tamarisk tree there, and there he called on the name of the Lord.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Abraham soɡe Filisti tena harɛ ta bela biɡri.
translated: Abraham lived with the Philistines in their land for a long time.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Waa nɔ bwa habɔɔ la, Korowii daah Abraham dɛ́ na ɡɛ waa dɛ yaradao. O yerɔ, “Abraham,” asɛ o sɛh ŋɔ “N Naa Korowii, menee nɔ.”
translated: When they had finished, God called Abraham from where they were praying. He called, "Abraham," and he answered, "My Lord God, here I am.

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Belaa o ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “Kpɔ he bibeŋkpoŋ Aesek haa dɛ nyin o waa la kaa Moria harɛ ta dɛ́ ka kpoo, dɛ́ fuuh ɛ saraha tɛ mɛ. Mɛɛ baɡlee bwejen bɛl haa ka ɛ saraha la o taa.”
translated: Belaa said to him, "Look, the ram that God provided for you to sacrifice on Mount Moriah, that one is gone, and its sacrifice is not there. But the young goat that you will sacrifice is there.

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Ate, o laa daa baa ka mar la kpɔ choɡ Aesek, dɛ ɡe kpɔ kere ne nii kpoŋe baa kaa.
translated: Ate, he will allow them to marry Aesek's daughter, and they will live together forever.

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: ásɛ a che ba nɛɛ ń tɛ nɛwɛnyina bwa hɛɛra. Mɛɛ ɛ ɡɛɛ dɛkalkɛ, he ɛ waa maa dɛ nyin.”
translated: If you do this, it will be good for you. But if you don't do it, you will not be well.

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: “N ɛ hɔɔh dɛ ba pu heyaa so, bonɔ laa n le. Anomanta, hé tɛ mɛ harɛ asɛ n yawɛ dɛ huɡe n haah.”
translated: I am afraid they will cause me trouble, because I am alone. However, he will not leave me to suffer and die.

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: dɛ soŋ ba ŋɔ, “Dɛ he sɛoh ŋɔ n huɡe n nɛn tenaa, amɔ he no n be, dɛ́ soŋe Soha bie Efron tɛ mɛ
translated: They said to him, "He saw that he had a lot of money, but he didn't want to," they said to Soha and Efron.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Ate, o soŋe Korowii ŋɔ, “N Naa Korowii n dɔɔ Abraham aa dɛ tomɛ, n dɛ soŋe, ɡel a chiɡ mɛ kɛrɛdɛ fini, dɛ́ teŋɛ nyoa haa baɡɛ n dɔɔ la ta.
translated: Then, he said to Korowii, "My Lord Korowii, you are the God of Abraham who is good, you are merciful, so that I may find favor in your sight, and that you may give me a child before I die.

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Asɛ bonɔɔ la chwa dɛ ka chemo dɛ ŋɔ, “N dɛ soŋe ɡel n nyɔ he nee nɔ tama ane.”
translated: When the woman finished cooking, she said to her husband, "I have finished eating, I am going to sleep now.

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Asɛ o ŋɔ, “Milka ne Neehɔ bie Bɛtoɛl bihaahnee men.”
translated: He said, "Milka and Neehɔ, Bɛtoɛl's people, helped us.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ba baa tɛɛ ba kondikwa, anee Abraham bonɔɔ la ŋɔ, “Maa di, nemenɛ maa bɔ me amaneɛ mɔɔ.” Asɛ Laban ŋɔ, “Amɔ bɔ he amaneɛ ɔ.”
translated: They came and stood together, and Abraham's son said, "My father, why do you cause me such trouble?" Then Laban said, "You are causing trouble!

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Belaa n ɡuŋi dɛ jam Korowii, dɛ kpɛɛ n dɔɔ Abraham Korowii waa kpɔ n naa andɛɛɡa kɛŋɔ ń ka mo n dɔɔ nime bie ba tɛ o bie la.
translated: And then he said to the people of Korowii, that Abraham Korowii would take his land and all the things he has and give them to another person who is not his relative

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Awola ba hɔɔh o ne baala baa ja o nɛɛ la kɛrɛdɛ. Ba chɔ bela jwaana la ne. Asɛ tɔ aa pera chɔɔ la, o soŋ ba ŋwɛnɛ, dɛ́ berɛ kaa o dɔɔ be.
translated: Awola was very happy with the children who were eating his food. They did that work well. After he finished the work, he gave them money and told them to come back.

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Belaa ba ta Rebɛka ne haah waa kaa chao, ne Abraham bonɔɔ la ne o bala la bwa ŋwɛnɛ.
translated: When Rebecca came to draw water, Abraham's servant had finished speaking.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Asɛ ba swee andɛɛɡa tɛ Rebɛka ŋɔ, “Ya nimambie, Korowii ɡel he ya kohona, he mun ya kohona aa wola nyoa dɔ, ásɛ he naanar yo lɛ ba dɔma bwɛɛre dɛɛ di a kpeɡri.”
translated: Then they went and told Rebekah, “Your brother Esau hates you and will kill you to avenge himself, so he will not let you escape from his hand.

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Bonɔɔ la bol wobɛlbwa waa kaa ɛ bwa chaɡle Aesek.
translated: Bonɔɔ went with the people and told them to tell Aesek.

ref: Abraham died at the ripe old age of
text: Dɛ o hwɛɛhrɔ kɛrɛdɛ, dɛ sewɛ dɛ ka pu o naawa.
translated: If he looks carefully, the thing cannot defeat him.

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: Asɛ Aesek so Gerar.
translated: And Isaac went to Gerar.

ref: Isaac planted grain and had a good harvest that same year. The Lord blessed him,
text: A bena la, Aesek parɔ dɛ dɔ kondikwaa kohona. Kwaa waa para la bele lafaa o dɔ dɛkalkɛ Korowii tɛo hɛɛra.
translated: And then, Aesek went into the forest to hunt. When he went there, he saw a large Korowii sleeping in a tree.

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Asɛ o berɛ ka kwaa pili kɔlɔŋa la baa kwia o mɛɛ bere saŋ mɔɔ o sewɛ la, dɛ berɛ paa sɔna la o mɛɛ fa aa paa la.
translated: When the forest became green, the baa tree would shed its leaves and the sewɛ tree would shed its leaves, but the dɛ tree would remain green and not shed its leaves.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: O berɔ le bela kwara dɛ ka kwi onfale, kɛ o te la, o ne nomɛlbwa ta o waa beɡrɛ kpa. Asɛ o pa o sɔn Rɛhɔbɔt. O munii bɔr dɔe. Dɛ ŋɔ, “Taraken Korowii tɛ yaa bɔr ya so dɛ́ ya.”
translated: He was very sad because he had no child, and when he died, his property would be lost. So he bore a son, Rehoboth. He named him because of this, saying, "The Lord has made room for us to live.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Ir la kondɛɛra la bini dɛ́ ka lawɛ bohloni anɛ aa wera ba tɛ mɛ, ásɛ n kpɔ daa he mɛɛ pɛla kondikwaa tɛo ɡɛ waa dɛ nyin.
translated: When the situation improves and they come out and are able to work, then I will see how the children will be able to live.

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Ate, o la ka lawɛ boohna la ba tɛ o nya, asɛ o kpɔ ɛ kondikwaa aa do som ɡɛ o mɛɛ aa dɛ nyin.
translated: Ate went to the market, and they didn't see him, but he saw a bird that was eating what he had planted in the ground.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: O kpɔ kondikonsoma la ne paanoo waa ɛa la do Jekɔb nɔn ta o ka tɛ o mɛɛ.
translated: He saw the chameleon and the lizard that were with Jacob so that he could eat them.

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Anee Jekɔb sɛh ŋɔ, “Menee he binomoa Esɔ, n ba ol la ne dɛ́ ba ɛ ɡɛ haa ŋɔa la. Ir so dɛ́ di kwaa la, ásɛ dɛ soŋe Korowii andɛɛɡa tɛ mɛ.”
translated: And Jacob said to him, "My lord, you know Esau, and when he comes with his men, he will kill us. If you go ahead, you will help God to save us.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Kórowii pili nyundua dɛ́ nɛ bwaala, dɛ́ tɛɛ hɛɛra he konduula bwa do wer, he dɔ kondikwaa ne senfala kohona.
translated: Kórowii went to the farm and to the market, and he returned to meet his friend to discuss the harvest.

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Belaa Aesek chaŋo ŋɔ, “N dɔɔ kpɔɔ ɛ he nomoa, dɛ kpɔ he lolanar bwa ɛ o bonɔɔna. N tɛo hɛɛra ásɛ o konduula bwa do wer o dɔ kondikwaa ne senfala kohona. Bɔkwaa kaa ń soŋe tɛɛ jaɔ?”
translated: Belaa Aesek said, "I want to see you in the morning, not to see you in the evening. I have heard that you are a good conductor and a good singer. What kind of songs do you sing?

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Esɔ toɔh marɛ o mɛɛ dɛ soŋo ŋɔ, “Amɔ wesoŋa beŋkpoŋ ɡboo he dɔ dɛ tɛ Jekɔb haa wola bɛlee dɔ tɛ n te kpa aa? Soŋe andɛɛɡa tɛ n ɡe.” Asɛ dɛ wi kohona.
translated: Esau then said to his father-in-law, "Why did you steal Jacob's heart and all that he has? Tell me the truth." But he refused.

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Waa dɛ kpɔ ɡɛ liire nɔ la, Rebɛka noɔ asɛ dɛ tomɛ ba ka yerɛ Jekɔb ba, dɛ ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “He dɔɔ Esɔ dɛ vɔrɔwe he vɔrɔwa kɛŋɔ wɛɛ kpoe.
translated: And when she saw that the children were grown up, Rebekah called her younger son, Jacob, and said to him, "Listen, your brother Esau is planning to kill you.

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Belaa Rebɛka ŋɔ Aesek be ŋɔ, “N sie aa Hiti bitoli nɔ nɛɛ lɛ. N kii ba kɔkɔkɔ. Dɛ Jekɔb ŋɔ wɛɛ mo ba bini bɛlee yaa, mɛɛ sewɔɔ hɛo.”
translated: And Rebekah said to Isaac, "I am tired of the Hittite women. I will die if this continues. If Jacob also takes a wife from the daughters of this land, what good is life to me?

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Esɔ nao kɛŋɔ Aesek swee andɛɛɡa tɛ Jekɔb, dɛ tao ŋwɛnɛ ŋɔ o kaa Mɛsopoteemea harɛ ka mo haah bela, dɛ ŋɔ o bɛ Keenan harɛ bihaah mo,
translated: God said to Isaac and to Jacob, and your descendants will be in Mesopotamia until they multiply, and they will come to Canaan until they fill it.

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Asɛ ɔmee lawɔ o ŋɔ, “Bonɔ ɔma kɛrɛdɛ! Bonɔ ɛɛ ɛ Korowii demii. Bonɔ ɛ doonyoa baa kpɔ o ta dɛɛ kaa nyundua.”
translated: Then he said to them, "Listen carefully! You are Korowii's child. You will never see your father again.

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Jekɔb ŋwɛnɛ waa valɔ la, o kpɔ sie chaɡɛ wee leebɔr dɛ kaa bela harɛ la ta.
translated: When Jacob finished working, he saw a big snake coming out from under the house.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Waa kaa tel kpɔɡ bini bobɛl la, o na kɔlɔŋ, asɛ peera aa wara kuore atoro chɔ baro. Gɛ kɔlɔŋ nɔ taa ba dɔ nee tɛa aa nyɔ. Bwe baa kpɔa tɔ o nyoa la kan kɛrɛdɛ.
translated: When you see a small bobɛl bird, it will build a nest and lay eggs among the thorny bushes. That nest cannot reach the ground. If it falls, it will fall to the ground unbroken.

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Asɛ Jekɔb ŋɔ ba be ŋɔ, “Wee ta nyɛɡre he ba boa peera nɔ nyoa la, hé tɛa nee a nyɔ dɛ berɛ kpɔa kaa dibɔr.”
translated: And Jacob's son said to him, "If you give me a wife and I have children with her, then I will not be a stranger in this land.

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Belaa ba chaŋe ŋɔ, “Yaa won ɛ ɡɛ, nemenɛ a bwa ba boa nyoa bonɔ, ásɛ ya ɡbili bwe nɔ le kɔlɔŋ nɔ nyoa mɔɔ dɛ́ tɛa nee a nyɔ.”
translated: Belaa said to them, "You know that when someone gives birth to a child, they will take care of it and raise it until it is grown.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Asɛ Laban ŋɔ, “Kasinteŋii, n lole tentenee hen.” Asɛ Jekɔb so o be korobibir bwa. Jekɔb tomɛ o naara dɛ kpɔ o bihaahna mo
translated: Then Laban said, "My kinsman, I have served you faithfully." Then Jacob also became very angry. Jacob sent his messengers to meet his brother.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Tɔpere la aa tela la, Jekɔb ŋɔ Laban be ŋɔ, “Bene anuanɛ la telo, tɛ mɛ n haah la ásɛ n mo name.”
translated: When they had finished, Jacob said to Laban, "Since my brothers have finished, what is my reward for what I have done?

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Kɛ a jwaana la, Leaa Laban berɛ lawɛ ba tɛ Jekɔb asɛ o ne o ka chɔ.
translated: When she gave birth, Leah and Laban's children told Jacob that he should leave.

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Jekɔb lawɛ Rakɛl ɡe o ne o chɔ. Dɛ o dɛ nyinu dɛ ɡwaa Lea dɛ koh a waa dɛ tomɛ Laban bene anuanɛ ja. Jekɔb bii wa
translated: Jacob and Rachel were his two wives. But he also had his wife Leah, who was Laban's older daughter and her younger sister. Jacob loved her.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Asɛ o kpoŋe tea dɛ lolɛ bibaa tɛ Jekɔb.
translated: He asked him to give tea for Jacob to drink all of it.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Rubin aa baa kpeɡ la, o la koo jaaɡe koma saŋ, dɛ ka kpɔ kwaane aa won ɡela baa ne haah ɛɛ nyin dɔŋa dɛ́ dɔ bii ba tɛ o nya Lea. Asɛ Rakɛl ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “Tɛ mɛ he bie kwaa nɔ ane.”
translated: Ruben went to lie with her, and he went to sleep with her, and when he woke up, he saw that it was Leah. Then Rachel said to her, "Give me some of your children.

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Asɛ Lea ŋɔ, “Lɛɛ haa lɛ n jaa nɔ ɡbo ta maɡra aa, he berɔ dɛɛ nyin he lɛ n bie kwaa nɔ o be ja aa?” Anee Rakɛl ŋɔ, “Dɛ hɛɛ tɛ mɛ he bie kwaa nɔ anee yaa, mɛɛ sɛoh dɛ tɛ he ne Jekɔb kalaŋre jwaana nɔ ne.”
translated: Then Leah said, "If you give me some of your mandrakes, why should I give you my husband to sleep with you?" And Rachel said, "Therefore, he will sleep with you

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Anomanta, o lolɛ bibaa dɛ ŋɔ, “Korowii tɛ mɛ bibaa dɛ laa sihiih kpɔ n sie.”
translated: Anomanta replied to his friend, "Korowii is not his friend, he has already killed him and you.

ref: But Laban told him, “If you really are my friend, stay on, and I'll pay whatever you ask. I'm sure the Lord has blessed me because of you.”
text: Gel n jemɛ kembɔr maa chɛ kɛ n kpɔ tɛɛ, ásɛ n nyin tɛɛ.”
translated: Gel and his friend Kembɔr were going to see the farm and clear it.

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Belaa Jekɔb ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “He ɡbabir jemɛ ɡɛ maa tomɛ ka tel, ne ɡɛ maa nyiŋi he kondɛɛra ta a do wer nyiŋa maa nyiŋi a ta kɛrɛdɛ la wa.
translated: Then Jacob said to him, "You will take my gift, and what I choose from the flock, if it is not spotted, I will not choose from the goats.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: A fa lɔ tamaa ken mɔɔ n ba, a berɔ ya kohona, asɛ Korowii tɛɛ hɛɛra bobɛlbwa maa dɛ la. Taraken, tɔpere telo kɛŋɔ n ɡbabir ɡe vɔrɔwe n dem.”
translated: When he came to the place where the people were, he saw a large crowd, and Korowii was giving a speech to them. Then, he took a stick and struck the ground with it.

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Anee kɛŋɔ, ɡel n ka nyiŋi he kondɛɛra la bwa bini fini dɛ́ lawɛ pebie bɛlbwa waa ɛ ombiŋi ne bombiire aa lɛɡra kɛɛ dɛ peeɡe la. Anee ɛ kembɔr maa dɛ nyin.
translated: And so, the child will grow in the womb for nine months until it is born, and whether it is a boy or a girl, it will be given a name. And it will be a joy for its mother.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Belaa Jekɔb teŋe daa ane kɛŋɔ sɛsɛ ne chɛnchɛne daa kpala, dɛ lɔ a pɛɡa peri ta, asɛ a to dɛ peeɡe.
translated: Jacob's father was sick and very old, and his eyes were dim, so he could not see clearly, and he could not distinguish them.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Jekɔb pere a bii la le anɔ la ta, dɛ ɡel anɔ la aa kaa la sie chaɡɛ Laban kwaa la waa paaɡe daa a sie la ne ambiŋere la be. Asɛ dɛ teɡɛ o ɡbabir kwaa la kaa baŋ bɛl, o ta an kpɔe koo Laban kwaa la ta.
translated: Jacob saw that the sheep were in heat, and that the sheep that were mating with Laban's flock were giving birth to striped and spotted ones. So he made his own flock strong, and he did not strengthen Laban's flock.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Anomanta, o tomɔ ba ka yerɛ Rakɛl ne Lea ŋɔ bá chemo kpɔɡ bini bela o ne o kondɛɛra aa lole la.
translated: Today, he remembered Rachel and Leah, the names of their children whom they named, and that he and his descendants will continue.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: Peera la teene kpoŋa saŋ la, n deero dɛ na kɛŋɔ bombala aa dɛ jweera la peeɡɔ dɛ lɛɡre.
translated: When the sun sets, your father will go to the farm and will return tomorrow morning.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jekɔb ta ɡelɛ Laban jemɛ kɛŋɔ o dɛ kaa o bwɛ.

translated: And at that time Judah said to him, "Please, my lord, I beg you, let your servant speak a word in your hearing, for you are like Pharaoh himself.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Dɛ berɛ lolɛ onɔ la jiseswɛm aa vɔwɛ o nɔn la. Asɛ ba pa o sɔn Sɛra.
translated: At that time when he was with Jesus and his people were with him. Then he gave birth to a son, Sarah.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ismael tena la kpɔ Josef kaa Ijipt, dɛ ka yoɡu tɛ Pɔtefa waa ɛ Ijipt kooro nɛra baa dɛ daahno la nomonome la bɛl.
translated: The Ishmaelites saw Joseph in Egypt, and they sold him to Potiphar, who was an Egyptian officer and the captain of the guard.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: O nomoa la ɡe nao kɛŋɔ Korowii teŋɛ o nɛ dɛ ɡel o wobɛlbwa do wer tɛo.
translated: The chief was very angry with Korowii because he did not listen to his words and did not obey him.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Pɔtefa kpɔ komɛlbwa do o nɔn ta dɛ laa liire kpɔ a ta. Kwaa waa dɛ lii a waa kondikwa waa di ɡbo. Dɛ Josef mao kɛrɛdɛ dɛ do wer bonɔɔ ɡe.
translated: Potiphar saw his wife holding his garment in her hand. And she said that he had tried to sleep with her. Joseph had fled and was not there.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Awola bɛl, Josef lɔ dem bela dɛɛ ɛ o toma. Dɛ dem bela bonɔɔna la bɛlbwa toole.
translated: After a while, Joseph and them were there and working. They were happy with their work.

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: Belaa haah la ta lawɛ o yal kpoŋe dɛ ŋɔ, “Gel n ne he chɔ.” O ta sɛɛh, dɛ yo lɛ o te dɛ chwa le buse, asɛ a ka o yal la do haah la nɔn ta.
translated: Belaa's mother went to call her son Kpong, saying, "Come and help me." He refused, and told her that he was busy, but he was the only son his mother had.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: dɛ ɡel ba ka lawɛ Josef ba do dea, bela kooro aa lawɛ nɛra dɛɛ do la, asɛ o chɔ bela.
translated: The woman was pregnant, Joseph had not known her, before they came together, she was found to be pregnant.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: asɛ dɛ laɡ ba do dea. Nomoa waa chɔ nɛra baa dɛ daah kooro dem la, o deemii o kpɔ ba do. A ɡɛ dea la binii ba lawɛ Josef ɡe do.
translated: They had been there for a long time. When he saw the people who were in the house, he thought he recognized them. It was the people who were with Joseph.

ref: and he asked, “Why are you so worried today?”
text: Asɛ o bɔɔ ba ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ he so sum ne ɡɛ nɔɔ?”
translated: Then he asked them, "What do you want to do with this?

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Anee Josef ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “Kenii he deera la mun ɛɛ baɡle. Daa kpala atoro la cheɡɔɔ tɛ tɔpera atoro.
translated: And Joseph said to him, "Kenii, you are mistaken. One side is not equal to the other.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Tɔpetooro wee la, kooro ɛɛ lee dɛ́ berɛ kaa he ne he tombena ta. Ásɛ he kpɔ kooro numol do o nɔn ta kɛŋɔ he fa aa dɛ doo sen la.
translated: When the sun rises, he will go to the farm to work for his family. So that he can get food for his mouth and clothes for his body to wear.

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Paanowɔɔra nomoa la aa noa kɛŋɔ Josef wono baɡle o dɔŋ la deera la mun tɛo a do som la, o bol o be ŋɔ, “N ɡe deero dɛ che paanoo wɛɡrɛre atoro.
translated: The chief priest had taken Joseph's brothers and put them in prison for three days, he said to them, "I am a God-fearing man.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: A habɔɔ tɔpetooro wee la, kooro la dɛ di o lola tɔpere, asɛ dɛ yerɛ o nomonome bwa. O le o sendoola nomoa la ne paanowɔɔra nomoa la, dɛ kpɔ ba ba o nɛra la sie.
translated: When the first rain falls, the ground becomes soft, and the weeds grow quickly. If you don't weed out the weeds and the grass, they will choke the crops.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: asɛ dɛ teŋe paanowɔɔra nomoa la nyu ɡɛ Josef aa bola la.
translated: When time had passed, everyone knew Joseph and called him.

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: o na nae anuanɛ aa do bwara, dɛ le moo la ta dɛ baa chwe kpɔɡa o nyoa.
translated: He will do good deeds and do good, if he does not, then he will fall into a water pit.

ref: Later, seven other heads of grain appeared, but they were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: Asɛ anfala anuanɛ ɡe berɛ sɛɡle daa la bampera. Peuwɔb bɛl tao le wee leebɔr dɛ ba chɔɡ a te, a ta wone ta pure.
translated: They will not be able to do anything until they are ready. They will be able to do the work with us, but it will not be perfect.
